prompt: How do I create a local model runtime with llamero?
completion: Use Llamero::Native::MLXRuntime.new(model_id: "mlx-community/Qwen3-0.6B-4bit"), then call runtime.start_session to get a ModelSession.

prompt: What class starts local inference in llamero?
completion: Llamero::Native::MLXRuntime. Create it with a model_id, then call start_session for a ModelSession.

prompt: How do I get a session for a local model in llamero?
completion: Call runtime.start_session on an MLXRuntime. It returns a Llamero::Native::ModelSession.

prompt: What must I call before chatting with a local llamero model?
completion: Call session.load_model first. Chatting before load_model raises SessionStateError.

prompt: Why does llamero raise SessionStateError saying the model is not loaded?
completion: You called chat before session.load_model. Always call session.load_model after start_session.

prompt: Do I need to load the model before each llamero chat call?
completion: No. Call session.load_model once; the model stays resident in memory for every later chat, stream, and structured call.

prompt: How do I send a chat message to a local model with llamero?
completion: session.chat([Llamero::Message.user("Hello!")]) returns a response; read the text with response.content.

prompt: How do I read the text of a llamero response?
completion: Use response.content. There is no response.text or response.message in llamero.

prompt: Does a llamero response have a .text method?
completion: No. The response text is response.content.

prompt: How do I stream tokens from a local model in llamero?
completion: session.chat_stream(messages) do |chunk| print chunk end. Each token is yielded to the block as it generates.

prompt: How do I stream a llamero cloud chat?
completion: client.chat_stream(messages) do |chunk| print chunk end. The text arrives only through the block.

prompt: How do I create messages in llamero?
completion: Use the helpers Llamero::Message.user("..."), Llamero::Message.system("..."), and Llamero::Message.assistant("...").

prompt: How do I make a system prompt message in llamero?
completion: Llamero::Message.system("You are a helpful assistant").

prompt: How do I get structured JSON output with llamero?
completion: Define a class inheriting Llamero::BaseGrammar with default values on every property, then call chat_structured(messages, YourClass) and read response.parsed.

prompt: What class do llamero structured output schemas inherit from?
completion: Llamero::BaseGrammar. Every property needs a default value, like property name : String = "".

prompt: Where is the parsed object after llamero chat_structured?
completion: In response.parsed. Use response.parsed.not_nil! after a successful call.

prompt: Do llamero BaseGrammar properties need default values?
completion: Yes. Every property in a Llamero::BaseGrammar subclass needs a default value, like property age : Int32 = 0.

prompt: How do I train a LoRA adapter with llamero?
completion: Build a Llamero::Native::TrainingDataset, add prompt/completion pairs, then call session.train_adapter("name", dataset, config). The model must be loaded first.

prompt: How do I fine-tune a local model with llamero?
completion: Use session.train_adapter("name", dataset, config) with a Llamero::Native::TrainingDataset of prompt/completion pairs. It trains a LoRA adapter on the resident model.

prompt: How do I teach a llamero model new facts?
completion: Create a TrainingDataset, add several phrasings of each fact with dataset.add(prompt, completion), then session.train_adapter("name", dataset, config) and activate the adapter.

prompt: How do I build a training dataset in llamero?
completion: dataset = Llamero::Native::TrainingDataset.new(system_prompt: "..."), then dataset.add("prompt", "completion") for each pair.

prompt: Does llamero support QLoRA?
completion: Yes, automatically. Training on a quantized base model like a 4-bit model uses QLoRA-style adapters; there is no separate mode to configure.

prompt: How do I run QLoRA training in llamero?
completion: Just call session.train_adapter on a 4-bit model. Quantized base models automatically get QLoRA-style adapters.

prompt: What learning rate should I use to memorize facts with llamero?
completion: Set config.learning_rate = 1e-4 with around 300 iterations. The default 1e-5 is conservative and learns slowly.

prompt: How do I configure llamero adapter training?
completion: config = Llamero::Native::AdapterTrainingConfig.new, then set fields like config.iterations = 300 and config.learning_rate = 1e-4 before passing it to train_adapter.

prompt: Where does llamero save trained adapters?
completion: In ~/.llamero/adapters/<name>/ as adapters.safetensors plus adapter_config.json, the standard mlx_lm format.

prompt: What format are llamero adapter artifacts?
completion: Standard mlx_lm format: adapters.safetensors and adapter_config.json in ~/.llamero/adapters/<name>/.

prompt: How do I activate an adapter in llamero?
completion: session.activate_adapters(Llamero::Native::AdapterStack.additive([Llamero::Native::AdapterSlot.new("name")])).

prompt: How do I turn an adapter off in llamero?
completion: Call session.deactivate_adapters. The knowledge switches off and the base model is untouched - it never reloads.

prompt: Does activating a llamero adapter reload the base model?
completion: No. Adapter activation and deactivation never reload the base model; session.load_count stays at 1.

prompt: Can I train a llamero adapter while another adapter is active?
completion: No. Training requires the plain base model; call session.deactivate_adapters first or you get AdapterTrainingError.

prompt: How many adapters can llamero activate at once?
completion: One at a time with scale 1.0 in the v1 bridge. Multiple slots or other scales raise AdapterActivationError.

prompt: How do I watch training progress in llamero?
completion: Pass a block to train_adapter: session.train_adapter(name, dataset, config) do |p| puts p.loss end. Validation loss streams as TrainingValidationEvent.

prompt: How do I check llamero training results?
completion: session.last_training holds the summary with final_loss, final_validation_loss, iterations, and total_time_ms.

prompt: How do I load a llamero training dataset from a file?
completion: Llamero::Native::TrainingDataset.from_pairs_jsonl(path) reads JSONL lines with prompt and completion fields.

prompt: What chat template does llamero training use by default?
completion: ChatML, which matches Qwen-family models. Pass a custom format proc to TrainingDataset.new for other model families.

prompt: How do I call OpenAI from Crystal with llamero?
completion: Subclass Llamero::Client with super(primary: :openai, fallbacks: [:anthropic]), or use Llamero::OpenAIClient.new directly. Set OPENAI_API_KEY in the environment.

prompt: Can I instantiate Llamero::Client directly?
completion: No, it is abstract. Subclass it and call super(primary: :openai, fallbacks: [...]) in initialize.

prompt: What providers does llamero support?
completion: OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, and OpenRouter (:openai, :anthropic, :groq, :openrouter), with automatic retry and failover between them.

prompt: How does llamero handle provider failures?
completion: Rate limits and 5xx errors retry with exponential backoff; auth errors fail over immediately to the next provider in your fallbacks list.

prompt: What environment variables does llamero need for cloud providers?
completion: OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, and OPENROUTER_API_KEY - only for the providers you list.

prompt: How do I build the llamero MLX bridge?
completion: Run ./build.sh in native/llamero-mlx (lib/llamero/native/llamero-mlx in a consuming project). It installs the dylib to ~/.llamero/lib/ once per machine.

prompt: How do I check if llamero is using real native inference?
completion: Check runtime.real_bridge?. When false, the Swift bridge is not built and llamero uses a deterministic mock.

prompt: Why does my llamero local model return canned mock text?
completion: The Swift MLX bridge is not built, so Bridge.auto fell back to the mock. Run build.sh in native/llamero-mlx and check runtime.real_bridge?.

prompt: Where does llamero download models to?
completion: Into ~/.llamero/models/, automatically on the first load_model call for a model_id.

prompt: How do I use a gated model like Gemma with llamero?
completion: Export HF_TOKEN with a Hugging Face token that has access. Ungated models like Qwen need no token.

prompt: What local model should I default to with llamero?
completion: mlx-community/Qwen3-0.6B-4bit - fast with good JSON compliance. Avoid SmolLM-135M for quality; its output is incoherent.

prompt: How do I remove Qwen3 think blocks from llamero output?
completion: response.content.gsub(/<think>.*?<\/think>/m, "").strip removes the <think> reasoning block.

prompt: What do I require to use llamero?
completion: Just require "llamero". Both the cloud clients and the native MLX track come from that one require.

prompt: How do I handle a llamero StructuredParseError?
completion: Rescue it and retry chat_structured - the model stays resident so retries are cheap. The raw model text is in error.raw_text.
